summary: Matrix (soundboard + audience) recording with clear first‑set sound; despite Jerry’s lyric miscues the show shines thanks to a blistering Shakedown opener and a soaring Might As Well.
review: The recording combines crisp soundboard audio on the first set with an audience‑sourced second half, yielding overall good fidelity. Musically the concert is praised for a high‑energy Shakedown Street opener, a standout Might As Well, and strong renditions of So Many Roads, China > Riders and SOTM, even though Jerry’s vocal lines slip occasionally. Fan sentiment is mixed but leans positive, reflecting both the occasional performance flaws and the many memorable moments.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (8 of 11) rate the show 4‑5 stars and highlight several standout songs; recording quality is consistently described as good to excellent, giving a weighted average in the 4‑star range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: First set captured from the soundboard – clear and balanced. Second set is an audience mix that adds ambience but remains listenable. No major dropouts reported, though a few reviewers note typical crowd noise.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set is shorter but praised for its tight playing and sound quality. The Shakedown Street opener sets a high‑energy tone, leading into a second set that builds to a powerful Might As Well closer, with several highlight jams in between.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals stumble on many lyrics but his guitar work is fiery and he sounds happy throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s presence lifts Shakedown Street; his rhythm/vocals are highlighted as a key element of the opener.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (and later Hornsby) receive praise for natural‑sounding tones and strong support throughout.
